data.title: BNB Drops Below 730 USDT with a 1.46% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Jul 19, 2025, 08:20 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has dropped below 730 USDT and is now trading at 729.700012 USDT, with a narrowed 1.46% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Data: USDC circulation increased by about 1.8 billion in the past 7 days
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to official data, in the 7 days ending July 17, Circle issued about 7.5 billion USDC, redeemed about 5.7 billion USDC, and the circulation increased by about 1.8 billion. The total circulation of USDC is 64.5 billion, and the reserve is about 64.7 billion US dollars, of which about 10.1 billion is in cash, and the Circle Reserve Fund holds about 54.7 billion US dollars.

data.title: A certain whale address increased its holdings by 805 ETH again 4 hours ago, and has bought 5578 ETH in this wave
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of Ai Yi, an analyst on the chain, the whale 0x54d once again increased its holdings of 805.1 ETH 4 hours ago, worth $2.894 million. A total of 5,578 ETH was bought in this wave, with a total value of $19.98 million and an average price of $3,583.

data.title: Circle's USDC Circulation Sees Significant Increase
data.description: According to PANews, official data reveals that Circle issued approximately 7.5 billion USDC and redeemed around 5.7 billion USDC in the seven days leading up to July 17. This activity resulted in a net increase of about 1.8 billion USDC in circulation. The total circulation of USDC now stands at 64.5 billion, with reserves amounting to approximately $64.7 billion. Of these reserves, around $10.1 billion is held in cash, while the Circle Reserve Fund holds approximately $54.7 billion.

data.title: ZachXBT questions Pepe creator's NFT project's slow action, hackers have transferred funds to MEXC
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Chain Detective ZachXBT published an article stating that the NFT project ChainSaw related to Pepe creator Matt Furie has been silent for more than a month since it was infiltrated by North Korean hackers. Although the stolen assets can be frozen, the team has not taken any countermeasures. At present, the North Korean hacker group ITW has transferred part of the funds to the MEXC exchange. It is reported that the incident caused a loss of more than 1 million US dollars.
